V-M-056.checks: Analytics service produces accurate summaries, top users, usage windows, runtime health, availability proof state, and observe-only abuse signals from M-054 data.
V-M-056.commands: pass-local 2026-05-20: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_analytics_service.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_alerts.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v
V-M-056.evidence: Analytics service tests cover summary/top users/timeseries/storage budget and high/critical alert handoff; scheduler maintenance regression passed.

verification-levels.module-local: Use deterministic M-054 fixtures to verify summaries, windowed rollups, top-user ordering, active sessions, stale telemetry, and observe-only abuse classifiers.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run after V-M-054/V-M-055 so analytics consumes the real planned persistence and runtime ingestion shapes.
verification-levels.phase-level: Admin API and UI gates cannot close unless M-056 returns all metrics requested by the operator without raw secrets or payload-level Telegram data.
verification-levels.phase-43-level: Phase-43 requires graph-ready timeseries, storage-budget summaries, high/critical abuse alert handoff to M-060, and no automatic enforcement.

phase-43-checks.check-1: Timeseries buckets are stable for hour/day ranges and include traffic, active connections, connections, duration, and errors.
phase-43-checks.check-2: Top users supports longer limits up to 100 and keeps deterministic ordering under ties.
phase-43-checks.check-3: High/critical abuse signals call M-060 alert creation once per dedupe key.
phase-43-checks.check-4: Storage-budget summary reports raw event, session, rollup, alert, and IP-observation retention windows.
phase-43-checks.check-5: Current/last IP summaries come from M-061 and are absent rather than guessed when source data is unavailable.

module-checks.check-1: Global summary returns issued proxy total, active/disabled/reissue_required counts, active connections, runtime health, and availability proof state.
module-checks.check-2: Per-assignment summary returns last_seen, traffic, duration, connection count, errors, active sessions, and current abuse signals.
module-checks.check-3: Day, week, month, and custom time windows calculate stable totals from rollups and raw recent events.
module-checks.check-4: Top users rank deterministically by traffic, duration, connection count, and error count with stable tie-breaking.
module-checks.check-5: Abuse signals detect too many IP hashes, too many concurrent sessions, traffic spikes, and repeated failures without disabling access.
module-checks.check-6: Missing or stale runtime telemetry is explicit and never rendered as false zero usage.
module-checks.check-7: Queries avoid N+1 scans for summary, top users, and per-assignment drill-down.

scenarios.scenario-1: Global summary returns issued proxy totals, active status counts, active connections, runtime health, and availability proof state.
scenarios.scenario-2: Per-assignment summary returns last seen, traffic, duration, connection count, errors, and active sessions.
scenarios.scenario-3: Top users ranking is stable for traffic, duration, connection count, and errors across selected time windows.
scenarios.scenario-4: Abuse signals detect many IP hashes, high concurrency, traffic spikes, and repeated failures without disabling access.
scenarios.scenario-5: Unknown/rejected SNI activity is visible globally but cannot be misattributed to a user.
scenarios.scenario-6: Phase-43 graph requests return bucketed data without scanning unbounded raw events.
scenarios.scenario-7: High/critical abuse creates an admin alert but does not disable proxy access automatically.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Analytics outputs include only internal ids, masked SNI, counters, states, and safe timestamps.
redaction-rules.rule-2: No Telegram chats, channels, message contents, contacts, Telegram user ids, full proxy links, or secrets are inferred or returned.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Pure service tests may use fixture-backed repositories and disposable databases.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Threshold tuning that would affect automatic enforcement remains out of scope; Phase-43 still requires admin-reviewed action.
